fields.name: DSC1 · Deer Stalking Certificate (Demo)
fields.rubric: A public demo of the Deer Stalking Certificate (Level 1) revision question bank. Anyone can study it — practice, flashcards, and mock exams — without an account.

fields.text: What are normally the last deer to change from winter coat into summer coat?
fields.option_c: Female deer

fields.text: Which male antlered deer usually cast their antlers first?
fields.option_c: Perruques

fields.text: Under what circumstances do female deer usually give birth to their young?
fields.option_a: In the middle of the herd for protection
fields.option_c: Out in the open where she can see danger approaching
fields.option_d: With the buck in close attendance waiting to breed afterwards

fields.text: The antler casting cycle is mainly governed by which hormone?
fields.option_c: Progesterone

fields.text: For a few days after their birth, young deer generally:
fields.option_a: Are left alone in cover by their mothers between suckling bouts
fields.option_b: Run with their mothers from an hour or two after birth
fields.option_c: Join in groups with other newly born deer
fields.option_d: Are led to low ground without delay

fields.text: A female deer will not conceive unless:
fields.option_a: She is in her home range
fields.option_b: She reaches a certain threshold body weight
fields.option_c: She is at least 4 years old
fields.option_d: She has been covered by at least two males

fields.text: What is the primary limiting factor of a deer's life span?
fields.option_c: Its teeth wearing out so that it cannot feed efficiently
fields.option_d: The strain of growing antlers or producing young

fields.text: A buck whose antlers in season are a spongy mass of velvet (a perruque head) should:
fields.option_a: Be culled as it may become infected and he will probably die
fields.option_b: Be protected and must not be shot
fields.option_c: Be preserved as good breeding stock
fields.option_d: Soon come into hard antler and produces a normal head

fields.text: Why do deer ruminate?
fields.option_b: It is essential to their digestion
fields.option_c: It is a reaction to stress

fields.text: What is the preferred habitat of most species of deer in the United Kingdom?
fields.option_c: Pasture

fields.text: The effect of rising numbers of deer in an area is likely to be first noticed through?
fields.option_a: Reduced diversity of plants at ground level and increased crop damage
fields.option_c: Increased antler quality

fields.text: How are deer wallows best described?
fields.option_c: Muddy and often scented with urine

fields.text: Deer under normal undisturbed conditions will:
fields.option_c: Feed in cycles of a few hours with peaks at dawn/dusk

fields.text: Deer are rarely seen to drink. This is because:
fields.option_a: They drink once every few days
fields.option_b: They get most of the water they need from their food
fields.option_c: In hot weather they stay in the shade

fields.text: Scent glands in most UK deer are located:
fields.option_a: Behind the ears and under the chin
fields.option_b: Between hooves, in corner of eye and outside hock
fields.option_c: All along the neck and spine

fields.text: Fraying is the term used to describe which activity of a male deer?
fields.option_a: Rubbing the bark from trees and shrubs with antlers
fields.option_b: Eating the bark of trees and shrubs
fields.option_c: Making territorial marks on the ground using hooves

fields.text: What is the term used to describe the covering of a deer's growing antlers?
fields.option_c: Velvet

fields.text: A reasonable estimation of the age class of a deer when it has been killed can be gained from:
fields.option_a: The number of points on its antler
fields.option_b: The amount of fat around its kidneys
fields.option_c: The amount of wear on its hooves
fields.option_d: The amount of wear on its teeth

fields.text: 'Browsing' is the term, which describes:
fields.option_a: Deer feeding on leaves and buds
fields.option_b: Deer feeding on grass and herbs
fields.option_c: Deer raiding cereal crops

fields.text: Where are a deer's antlers shed and regrown from each year?
fields.option_c: The pedicle

fields.text: Which of the following descriptions apply to deer droppings?
fields.option_a: Often black or dark brown, shiny when fresh often with ends pointed or indented
fields.option_c: Always round and fibrous
fields.option_d: Pale in colour and similar to small fox droppings

fields.text: After the shot, a deer hunches its back and walks slowly into cover. This is a typical reaction from an animal, which has been hit in:
fields.option_c: The gut

fields.text: If you suspect that you have wounded a deer and it has disappeared from sight, what should you do?
fields.option_a: Wait for 10-15 minutes before going forward to the place where it was standing when you shot
fields.option_b: Wait for 1 minute before going forward to the place it was standing when shot
fields.option_c: Go straight to where you last saw it before it disappeared
fields.option_d: Go round behind where you last saw it and approach the place from there

fields.text: After the shot, the deer kicks out its hind legs and rushes into cover with neck outstretched. Traces of pink and frothy blood are found. Where has it been hit?
fields.option_c: The liver

fields.text: After a shot, the deer falls down and gets up almost immediately or staggers before rushing away. Only a few drops of blood, some short hair and a long splinter of bone is found. Where has it been hit?
fields.option_c: The leg

fields.text: You are stalking in woodland and spot a deer from a range of about 40 metres behind a large tree. All you can see is the animal's head looking sideways. What is the recommended procedure for taking a shot?
fields.option_b: Wait until it exposes the chest area and take a heart/lung shot
fields.option_c: Shoot at a point half way between the eye and base of the ear
fields.option_d: Make a noise so it looks at you before shooting it between the eyes

fields.text: Which is the best point of aim to ensure a humane kill at most sensible distances?
fields.option_b: Half way up the body just behind the shoulder
fields.option_c: High in the neck

fields.text: If you intend to shoot one deer, how much ammunition should you carry whilst stalking?
fields.option_a: One round loaded in the rifle
fields.option_b: One round loaded in the rifle and one in the pocket
fields.option_c: One round loaded in the rifle and one in the magazine
fields.option_d: One round loaded in the rifle, more than one in the magazine and some spare in the pocket

fields.text: What is the main purpose of stalking to within easy range of a deer?
fields.option_b: To check the quality of the antlers before taking the shot
fields.option_c: To test the stalkers ability to get in close
fields.option_d: To increase the chances of spotting an additional shootable deer

fields.text: What is the advantage of a telescopic sight?
fields.option_a: Very long shots can be taken with ease
fields.option_b: You can aim more precisely at all ranges
fields.option_c: You do not need to carry binoculars to identify the deer
fields.option_d: It enables you to see and shoot the deer through undergrowth

fields.text: Within woodlands, at what range are the majority of deer shot?
fields.option_c: 150-200 metres

fields.text: A wounded stag is struggling hard but seems unable to run away and could possibly be approached. Should it be?
fields.option_a: Returned to later, accompanied by a dog
fields.option_b: Shot again if safe to do so
fields.option_c: Left to die in peace
fields.option_d: Held down to be finished off with a knife

fields.text: When a deer drops instantly to the shot, what should you do?
fields.option_a: Rush up to it ready to finish it off if it's not dead
fields.option_b: Immediately shoot at it again where it lies
fields.option_c: Remain where you are, ready to shoot again and observe the animal
fields.option_d: Unload and prepare to gralloch because it is dead for sure

fields.text: After successfully shooting a deer from a group, what should you do?
fields.option_a: Stay still until the remaining deer in the immediate area have moved out of sight
fields.option_b: Stand up and frighten the rest of the deer away quickly
fields.option_c: Rush up and bleed the carcass before the heart has stopped beating
fields.option_d: Drag the carcass away from the other deer as soon as possible

fields.text: Which of the following factors are most important for safety in the choice of a stalker's knife?
fields.option_c: Appearance
fields.option_d: Serrated blade for cutting through bone

fields.text: Which of the following items is the most important for a sensible stalker to carry with him whilst out stalking?
fields.option_c: A bottle of gun oil

fields.text: What is the advantage in using a high seat?
fields.option_a: The deer may not see the stalker
fields.option_b: The deer may not smell the stalker
fields.option_c: The shot is likely to be safer than one taken from ground level

fields.text: When stalking alone on leased ground, which of the following might you carry?
fields.option_c: Permission to stalk signed by the landowner/occupier or holder of the stalking right

fields.text: The use of a stick (or split sticks) as a stalking aid is:
fields.option_a: Sensible because it makes shooting and spying steadier
fields.option_b: Unsporting because it makes shooting too easy
fields.option_c: Unnecessary because it is easier and quicker to shoot freehand
fields.option_d: Tiring because it is one more heavy and awkward thing to carry

fields.text: You are on a steep bank above a deer, which shooting position is likely to be the most comfortable and stable?
fields.option_c: Freehand

fields.text: When should the bolt be removed from the rifle whilst stored in the home?
fields.option_a: All the time to avoid its use by an unauthorised person e.g. child/thief
fields.option_b: Only when you wish to clean the bolt
fields.option_c: Only when necessary to avoid a build-up of condensation
fields.option_d: Occasionally to ease pressure on the striker spring

fields.text: When should you take off the safety catch whilst deer stalking?
fields.option_a: When you load the rifle and set off with it safely slung over your shoulder
fields.option_b: When you start the final approach on a stalk
fields.option_c: When you enter thick cover and silence is essential
fields.option_d: When you are in the aim and about to squeeze the trigger

fields.text: Someone offers you a rifle with the bolt closed. What should you do?
fields.option_a: Refuse to accept the rifle until he/she proves that it is unloaded
fields.option_b: Take the rifle and ask him/her if it is loaded before pulling the trigger to check
fields.option_c: Point it in a safe direction and pull the trigger to check
fields.option_d: Open the bolt and look inside to prove it empty

fields.text: When shooting deer in woods or on level ground safety can be improved by?
fields.option_a: Lying down to take every shot
fields.option_c: Only shooting at deer with a screen of bushes behind them to break up the bullets
fields.option_d: Only shooting into open spaces e.g. down rides

fields.text: If you accidentally plug your barrel with peat or snow, what should you do?
fields.option_a: Fire a shot to clear it
fields.option_b: Carry on and take more care of your rifle
fields.option_c: Unload all cartridges at once and properly clean the bore
fields.option_d: Push the obstruction down the barrel as far from the muzzle as possible

fields.text: When approaching a deer you have shot and that you believe to be dead, what should your first action be?
fields.option_a: Stick a knife into the base of the neck to bleed it
fields.option_b: Make sure it is dead by touching an eyeball and watching for 'eye-blink' reaction
fields.option_c: Grab the back leg to restrain it in case it is still alive
fields.option_d: Shoot it in the head to make sure it is dead

fields.text: If you have to shoot an injured deer at close range, what should you do?
fields.option_a: Lie down to ensure a steady shot at the neck outlined clearly against the sky
fields.option_b: Get close enough so you can rest the rifle muzzle against the deer's skin
fields.option_c: Ensure there is a soft safe backstop behind the deer to stop the bullet
fields.option_d: Get someone to hold a leg to keep the animal still

fields.text: A safe backstop is required before you take a shot at a deer. What is the best form of backstop?
fields.option_a: Clear air, i.e. a silhouette target with nothing behind the deer
fields.option_b: A dense screen of bushes and scrub which will absorb the bullet
fields.option_c: A belt of trees or a thick plantation
fields.option_d: A rising bank of soft ground

fields.text: When stalking deer in thick cover should you?
fields.option_a: Use a slow, heavy calibre bullet to cut through the twigs and underbrush
fields.option_b: Use a fast light calibre bullet to cut through the twigs and underbrush
fields.option_c: Use solid (full metal jacket) bullets to avoid bullet break-up on twigs
fields.option_d: Not take any shots at deer that are not fully visible and without a safe backstop

fields.text: What is the maximum distance that a bullet from a deer rifle might travel if there is no solid backstop?
fields.option_c: 5500-6000 metres

fields.text: When crawling towards deer with a companion what best describes the recommended condition for the rifles?
fields.option_a: Only the leader's rifle should be loaded and pointing forwards
fields.option_b: Both rifles should be loaded in case other deer appear behind you
fields.option_c: Neither rifle should be loaded until the firing position is reached
fields.option_d: Only the second person should have a loaded rifle, the leader should be selecting the route

fields.text: Stalking in fog is:
fields.option_a: Easy because you can approach the deer unseen
fields.option_b: Dangerous because you cannot be sure of a safe backdrop
fields.option_c: Safer because walkers will not be on the hill
fields.option_d: More exciting because you cannot be sure where the deer are

fields.text: Carrying a rifle with the firing pin resting on a chambered round is:
fields.option_a: Always dangerous and must never be done
fields.option_c: Acceptable if the rifle is in its case
fields.option_d: Acceptable if the safety catch is applied thus locking the bolt

fields.text: Before you climb into a high seat what should you do?
fields.option_a: Ensure that the rifle is loaded in case you see a deer as you climb the ladder
fields.option_b: Cut some branches to improve your concealment as you climb
fields.option_c: Check the ladder and seat for damage and unload your rifle before climbing into the seat
fields.option_d: Rest the loaded rifle against the base of the seat and pull it up on a cord when you are safely seated

fields.text: In England and Wales what is the minimum muzzle energy required from a rifle to be legal for stalking all species of deer?
fields.option_c: 1750 ft/lbs (2373 joules)

fields.text: In England and Wales which of the following rifle and ammunition combinations Is ILLEGAL for use to kill deer?
fields.option_a: .222 Remington 45gr hollow point bullet (ME: 1000 ft lbs/1356 joules)
fields.option_b: .243 Winchester 100gr soft nose bullet (ME: 2090 ft lbs/2831 joules)
fields.option_c: 30-30 Winchester 170gr soft nose bullet (ME: 1765 ft lbs/2397 joules)
fields.option_d: 30-06 Springfield 180gr soft nose bullet (ME: 2914 ft lbs/3948 joules)

fields.text: In England and Wales it is illegal to shoot any deer at night unless injured. How is "night" defined in law?
fields.option_a: When you cannot read a car number plate at 25 metres
fields.option_b: Between one hour after sunset and one hour before sunrise
fields.option_c: During official "lighting up time" as published in the local newspapers
fields.option_d: Between 2000 hours GMT and 0800 hours GMT

fields.text: Red stags are grazing on the edge of an airfield during the hours of darkness. The deer pose a threat to public safety if they are not dealt with. Who in England would issue a night shooting licence:
fields.option_c: Natural England

fields.text: If you wish to net or trap deer in England from whom, is permission obtained?
fields.option_a: You can net or trap wild deer without permission
fields.option_b: The owner of the land where the deer are to be trapped
fields.option_c: The Forestry Commission

fields.text: If you wish to net or trap deer in Wales from whom, is permission obtained?
fields.option_a: You can net or trap wild deer without permission
fields.option_b: The owner of the land where the deer are to be trapped
fields.option_c: The Forestry Commission

fields.text: What is the minimum legal calibre for a roe deer rifle in England and Wales?
What is the minimum legal calibre for a muntjac and CWD deer rifle in England and Wales?
In England, where would you go to for the definitive up to date male Roe season?
fields.option_c: Natural England
fields.option_d: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In Wales, where would you go to for the definitive up to date male Red season?
fields.option_c: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In Wales, where would you go to for the definitive up to date male Fallow season?
fields.option_c: NatureScot
fields.option_d: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In England, where would you go to for the definitive up to date male Sika season?
fields.option_b: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
fields.option_c: NatureScot

fields.text: In Wales, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Red hind season?
fields.option_c: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In England, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Sika hind season?
fields.option_c: Natural England
fields.option_d: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In Wales, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Fallow doe season?
fields.option_c: NatureScot
fields.option_d: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In England, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Roe doe season?
fields.option_b: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
fields.option_c: NatureScot

fields.text: In England, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Chinese Water Deer doe season?
fields.option_c: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In England and Wales in certain circumstances shotguns firing non-spherical projectiles (slug) weighing more than 350 grains or certain shot may be used to kill deer which are not injured but doing serious damage to crops. Which of the following may be used?
fields.option_a: A 20 bore shotgun or larger with AAA shot or legal slug
fields.option_b: A 12 bore shotgun or larger with SSG shot only
fields.option_c: A 12 bore shotgun or larger with AAA shot only or legal slug
fields.option_d: A .410 bore but with slug only

fields.text: Under what circumstances is it legal for an occupier to shoot uninjured deer out of season in England?
fields.option_a: If you were unable to do so in the open season due to illness (certified by a doctor)
fields.option_b: If deer of the same species are seriously damaging your agricultural crops and you cannot prevent further damage by other means, and you shoot them where the damage is occurring
fields.option_c: If the game keeper would not let you do so in the open season due to risk of disturbing the pheasant shooting
fields.option_d: If they are eating all the bluebells and other wild flowers

fields.text: In England what type of appropriate calibre rifle bullets may legally be used to kill deer?
fields.option_a: Any soft nosed or hollow nosed bullet
fields.option_c: Any bullet with a metal jacket totally covering the lead core
fields.option_d: Only a bullet over 100 grains with a metal jacket totally covering the lead core

fields.text: Shooting deer from a vehicle in England & Wales is?
fields.option_b: Only legal from a stationary vehicle with the engine switched off
fields.option_c: Legal at night without the occupier's written permission but with a DEFRA permit

fields.text: Which of the following is illegal to use for shooting muntjac in England & Wales?
fields.option_a: A .222 and 50 grain soft nosed ammunition
fields.option_b: Any rifle and bullet combination legal for other species of deer
fields.option_c: A .22 LR with hollow-point bullets
fields.option_d: A 5.56 mm using 50 grain soft-nosed or hollow-point bullets

fields.text: You stalk a roe doe and her buck kid during January in England. You intend to cull the doe can you also cull the buck kid?
fields.option_a: No roe bucks are out of season in January
fields.option_b: No unless you have a license to shoot out of season
fields.option_c: Yes if the landowner tells you too
fields.option_d: Yes the law allows dependent young to be culled in such circumstances

fields.text: In Scotland, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Red hind season?
fields.option_c: NatureScot
fields.option_d: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In Scotland, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Sika hind season?
fields.option_c: Natural England
fields.option_d: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In Scotland, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Fallow doe season?
fields.option_c: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

fields.text: In Scotland, where would you go to for the definitive up to date Roe doe season?
fields.option_b: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
fields.option_c: Natural Resources Wales

fields.text: To sell venison legally in Scotland, a person who is not a licensed venison dealer:
fields.option_a: Can only sell to licensed Venison dealer
fields.option_b: Must own the land where it was shot and keep records
fields.option_c: May sell it to an hotel or restaurant or butchers shop even if it does not have a venison dealer's license
fields.option_d: May barter it for something else

fields.text: Which is the statutory body responsible for the conservation and control of deer in Scotland?
fields.option_c: The Association of Deer Management Groups

fields.text: What is the minimum legal muzzle velocity allowed for a bullet to shoot deer in Scotland?
fields.option_c: 1900 ft/sec

fields.text: What is the minimum legal bullet weight for use on sika deer in Scotland?
fields.option_c: 80 grains

fields.text: What is the minimum legal muzzle energy of a bullet for use on red deer in Scotland?
fields.option_c: 1750 ft/lbs

fields.text: Shooting any species of deer in Scotland is allowed on a Sunday:
fields.option_b: Only if the shooting would be legal on any other day of the week
fields.option_c: Only if authorised by a Church Official
fields.option_d: Only with permission from the Police

fields.text: Under what circumstances may a deer be killed in England, Scotland and Wales with a .22 rimfire without breaking the law?
fields.option_a: When it is marauding in a suburban garden
fields.option_c: At night using a spotlight
fields.option_d: When injured to prevent further suffering

fields.text: If serious deer damage to crops or trees is being suffered by foresters or tenant farmers on fenced land in Scotland, what are their legal options?
fields.option_a: They can shoot deer at night without permission
fields.option_b: They can shoot deer at night with the landowner's written permission
fields.option_c: They can apply to NatureScot for a night shooting permit
fields.option_d: They can trap or snare the deer concerned because shooting at night is not allowed

fields.text: In Scotland the use of a Thermal Imaging or Image Intensifying sight at night is
fields.option_c: Legal with authorisation from NatureScot
fields.option_d: Legal if you can't get out during daylight hours

fields.text: In Scotland a Thermal Imaging or Image Intensifying sight
fields.option_a: Can only be used within the hours defined as daylight
fields.option_b: Can be used at night with authorisation from NatureScot
fields.option_c: Is illegal only Thermal Imaging or Image Intensifying spotting scopes are allowed
fields.option_d: Can be used at night with permission from the landowner

fields.text: Deer marauding into crops in Scotland may legally be?
fields.option_b: Shot using any firearm/ammunition legal for that species under normal circumstances
fields.option_c: Shot using any shotgun and size of shot
fields.option_d: Shot using a 12 bore loaded with a single spherical ball

fields.text: As a land owner or holder of the shooting rights, you can release big red stags on your land to improve the wild stock?
fields.option_c: Anywhere except the Hebrides and some Western Isles

fields.text: Who issues Firearms Certificates in Scotland?
fields.option_c: Police Scotland

fields.text: In England, Scotland and Wales if a deer has been injured on the road?
fields.option_a: It may be killed at night or out of season without permission by any means
fields.option_b: Only a RSPCA officer may kill it out of season
fields.option_c: The police must be called to authorise its humane dispatch at night
fields.option_d: Only the landowner may kill it

fields.text: You are visiting an estate in Scotland to stalk Stags in August. The stalker tells you to shoot a red hind (out of season) because it is marauding on some turnips:
fields.option_a: You may do so only if it is actually in the turnip field
fields.option_b: You may only do so if you are specifically named on a NatureScot authorisation
fields.option_c: You may only do so if you have actually seen the damage yourself and judge it to be serious
fields.option_d: It would be legal for a paying guest to do so under the stalkers guidance

fields.text: What is the minimum bullet weight, which may be legally used to shoot roe deer only in Scotland?
fields.option_c: 90 grains

fields.text: What is the minimum legal muzzle energy allowed for a bullet to shoot roe deer only, in Scotland?
fields.option_c: 1500 ft/lbs

fields.text: Your deer rifle needs adjusting by a gunsmith but you are busy. What are your legal options?
fields.option_a: You can tell your partner to take it in as long as he/she is over 17 and takes your Firearm Certificate with him/her
fields.option_b: You can send it by normal parcel post
fields.option_c: You can send it by a firearms registered carrier
fields.option_d: You can tell anyone over 21 to take it in as long as they have your FAC with them and your instructions in writing

fields.text: Why have muntjac no legal Close Season in Britain?
fields.option_a: They have the status of pests and must be shot on sight
fields.option_b: They breed all the year round and therefore a Close Season will not protect new-born fawns
fields.option_c: You cannot tell the sexes apart so could not obey a Close Season
fields.option_d: The fawns are self sufficient from birth therefore do not need parental support

fields.text: Under what circumstances can you have a rifle and ammunition for it in your car on the public road?
fields.option_a: At any time in case you find a deer injured by a traffic accident
fields.option_b: If you can prove you have lawful authority and reasonable excuse
fields.option_c: If you are visiting a farm where you hope to get permission to stalk
fields.option_d: If you have the bolt removed from the rifle

fields.text: What is the legal position in regard to shooting deer with a bow and specially designed hunting arrows?
fields.option_a: It is not legal anywhere in UK
fields.option_b: It is only legal in an enclosed deer park
fields.option_c: It is legal in Scotland but not England or Wales
fields.option_d: It is legal for foreigners with a permit from the Tourist Board

fields.text: Who owns wild deer while they are alive?
fields.option_a: The owner of the land on which they are found
fields.option_c: The holder of the deer stalking rights over the land on which they are found

fields.text: From where would you obtain written permission to shoot deer on land which does not belong to you?
fields.option_c: Holder of the deer stalking rights

fields.text: If you are unable to produce your Firearm Certificate when a Police Officer asks you for it?
fields.option_a: He/she may legally seize and hold your firearms until you produce it
fields.option_b: He/she may require that you produce your certificate at a police station within 24 hours
fields.option_c: You are liable to prosecution for failure to carry a valid certificate with you
fields.option_d: He/she must accept a photocopy of your certificate instead

fields.text: In the UK where may you release sika into the wild?
fields.option_c: Not at All
fields.option_d: Under license, but excluding the Western Isles

fields.text: Which of the following types of full bore rifle is illegal in the United Kingdom?
fields.option_c: Bolt action rifle

fields.text: Your stalking partner has asked you to store his rifle in your gun cabinet whilst he is on holiday, because there have been a number of burglaries in the area. What is the legal position?
fields.option_a: You may do so for up to three weeks
fields.option_b: You may do so only if you inform the police by registered letter within seven days
fields.option_c: You may not do so only if he removes the bolt so that you cannot use the rifle illegally
fields.option_d: You may do so only if your FAC has his rifle entered on it

fields.text: Your 14 year old son passed the written assessment of the Deer Stalking Certificate (Level One) and shoots well with a .22" in the Cadet Corps. He now wants to go deer stalking but has no Firearm Certificate. What are your legal options? (not used In Northern Ireland)
fields.option_a: He can use your rifle, once he has first obtained a Firearms Certificate for that rifle
fields.option_b: You can lend him your rifle without a Certificate as long as you supervise him closely
fields.option_c: You can allow him to buy a rifle himself having first obtained a Firearms Certificate
fields.option_d: You send him out under supervision of a local game keeper whose estate rifle he will borrow

fields.text: The holder of a Firearm Certificate may legally:
fields.option_a: Purchase a rifle or ammunition when they are 14
fields.option_b: Purchase a rifle or ammunition when they are 16
fields.option_c: Purchase a rifle or ammunition when they are 17
fields.option_d: Purchase a rifle or ammunition when they are 18

fields.text: A heart shot deer runs 80 metres before falling dead in the middle of a neighbour's field. What are your legal options?
fields.option_a: You can retrieve it without asking permission
fields.option_b: You can retrieve it without asking permission, but only if you leave your rifle on your side of the boundary

fields.text: When do most mature Muntjac bucks cast their antlers?
fields.option_c: April/May

fields.text: A muntjac buck's first antlers
fields.option_a: Always have small coronets and brow tines
fields.option_b: Are usually simple thin spikes with no coronets or brow tines
fields.option_c: Are always long thick spikes with no brow tines
fields.option_d: Are cast on a date depending on the animals date of birth

fields.text: Muntjac are:
fields.option_b: Usually seen in ones and twos
fields.option_c: Usually seen in small herds

fields.text: Which of these male deer might be seen mating when they have just cast their antlers?
fields.option_c: Muntjac

fields.text: How often can muntjac females bare a fawn?
fields.option_b: Twice a year in June and December
fields.option_c: Every three months

fields.text: What noise do mature muntjac make when suspicious or disturbed?
fields.option_c: A single deep bark

fields.text: What is the typical antler configuration for the adult male muntjac?
fields.option_c: Short hooked antlers

fields.text: What is the normal period between the birth of a muntjac fawn and the end of lactation?
fields.option_c: About 7 months

fields.text: A small deer runs away from you in thick cover with its tail straight up in the air the underside is pure white. Which species is it likely to be?
fields.option_c: Chinese water deer

fields.text: Why is a heavily pregnant female muntjac an ideal class to cull?
fields.option_a: Because they will not have a dependant fawn
fields.option_b: Because their venison is the most tender
fields.option_c: Because they are less wary so easier to stalk
fields.option_d: Because they spend more time in the open

fields.text: Which species of deer breed regularly throughout the year?
fields.option_c: Sika

fields.text: How would you describe the colour of an adult muntjac in summer coat?
fields.option_a: It is heavily spotted all over
fields.option_b: It has a row of spots either side of the spine
fields.option_c: It has a dark grey coat with black markings
fields.option_d: It is a chestnut colour brown with white under parts

fields.text: During which period are muntjac born?
fields.option_c: April-May

fields.text: During which period are muntjac bucks fertile?
fields.option_c: December-March

fields.text: A muntjac seen in velvet in December is probably?
fields.option_c: In poor health

fields.text: Which species of deer in UK has black lines and bony ridges running from its eyes to its antler bases?
fields.option_c: Chinese water deer

fields.text: During which period of the year are muntjac antlers normally cast?
fields.option_c: April/May

fields.text: Which of these species has the most limited distribution in Britain?
fields.option_c: Muntjac

fields.text: Which species of deer are associated with reed-bed habitats?
fields.option_c: Chinese water deer

fields.text: Which species of deer has hind legs that appear to be longer than their forelegs?
fields.option_c: Chinese water deer

fields.text: Which species of male deer has prominent tusks and no antlers?
fields.option_c: Chinese water deer

fields.text: Which species of deer has scent glands in the groin (inguinal glands)?
fields.option_c: Chinese water deer

fields.text: A deer is described to you as having black button eyes and nose with round ears, resembling a teddy bear. What species of deer is it likely to be?
fields.option_c: Muntjac deer

fields.text: During which month do Chinese water deer rut?
fields.option_c: October

fields.text: Chinese water deer usually give birth during the period of?
fields.option_c: August/September

fields.text: Which species of deer often produce "multiple births" of three or more fawns?
fields.option_c: Muntjac deer

fields.text: Chinese water deer does may have their first fawns at the age of?
fields.option_c: Two years old

fields.text: You have been instructed to immediately cull a deer on a field which has recently been spread with slurry. What would you do to comply with food hygiene regulations?
fields.option_a: Shoot and gralloch the animal in the field and sell to the game dealer as normal
fields.option_b: Shoot the deer but reject the carcass because of potential contamination
fields.option_c: Cull the deer and wash the carcass down to make it fit to go in the food chain
fields.option_d: Wait until the deer has moved off the field, then cull it and treat it as normal

fields.text: Ideally, in the field you should try to hang a deer when gralloching because?
fields.option_a: It stops you getting back ache
fields.option_b: Reduces the risk of environmental contamination
fields.option_c: Reduces risk of fly strike
fields.option_d: Encourages external parasites to drop off naturally

fields.text: Shot game that enters a water course is:
fields.option_b: Safe as any contamination will wash off
fields.option_c: Likely to have bacteria transmitted to the carcass
fields.option_d: Safe as they are classed as potable water

fields.text: Water required to keep a game larder and utensils clean can come from:
fields.option_c: Any spring or stream

fields.text: Soil and plant debris from the countryside:
fields.option_a: Can contaminate game meat with bacteria
fields.option_b: Is what makes game organic and is not dangerous
fields.option_c: Cannot transfer disease to meat or other animals
fields.option_d: Can make game gritty to eat but is not dangerous

fields.text: You are asked to collect game carcasses with your vehicle which has been carrying a chemical sprayer, a chainsaw and petrol you should:
fields.option_a: Thoroughly wash your vehicle out then collect the game
fields.option_b: Put the game on some fresh leaves or straw
fields.option_c: Just put it in the back of the vehicle regardless
fields.option_d: Put the game in plastic bags in the back of the vehicle

fields.text: Before loading carcasses into the back of a pick-up you should:
fields.option_a: Put a good layer of straw down
fields.option_b: Clean out the back and sterilise
fields.option_c: Use clean hessian or plastic seed sacks to absorb the blood
fields.option_d: Push the spare petrol can aside so you can load dogs and the game

fields.text: You shoot 3 Fallow deer and 2 Muntjac on a morning stalk. Can you transport all of the deer together in your vehicle?
fields.option_a: Yes as long as you apply hygienic practices for transporting carcasses
fields.option_b: No species must be separated for transporting
fields.option_c: No not in the same vehicle as there is a risk of cross contamination
fields.option_d: As long as your game dealer agrees

fields.text: You shoot several ducks of different species on a morning flight. Can you transport all of the carcasses together in your vehicle?
fields.option_a: Yes as long as you apply hygienic practices for transporting carcasses
fields.option_b: No species must be separated for transporting
fields.option_c: No not in the same vehicle as there is a risk of cross contamination
fields.option_d: As long as your game dealer agrees

fields.text: The rule of storing fuel in a game larder is:
fields.option_a: It is fine to do so if there is no game in the larder at the same time
fields.option_b: The fuel is kept away from any game hanging up
fields.option_c: You scrub out the game larder properly afterwards before using it for game
fields.option_d: You should never store fuel in the game larder

fields.text: A carcass stored in a garden shed for two days will be?
fields.option_b: Better tasting than one in a chiller
fields.option_c: Worth less when offered for sale

fields.text: What is a zoonosis?
fields.option_a: A disease transmittable from wild animals to cattle
fields.option_b: A disease transmittable from plants to animals
fields.option_c: A disease transmittable from mammals to birds
fields.option_d: A disease transmittable from animals to humans

fields.text: Identify the zoonotic diseases from the groups listed below:
fields.option_a: Yellow fever, Diphtheria, Polio, Cholera and Plague.
fields.option_b: Foot rot, arthritis, dermatitis, sway back and laminitis:
fields.option_c: Ticks, Keds, Lice, Warble fly and Nasal Bot fly
fields.option_d: TB, Rabies, Brucellosis and Avian influenza

fields.text: Which of the following notifiable diseases can be transmitted to humans from animals?
fields.option_a: Liver fluke, lung worm, nasal bot, warble
fields.option_c: Measles

fields.text: If bacteria from the environment get into game meat it could?
fields.option_a: Be detected and the meat thrown away
fields.option_c: Cause serious food poisoning
fields.option_d: Only harm the young and vulnerable

fields.text: What is the most suitable material for lining a game larder?
fields.option_c: Smooth, light in colour, impervious materials

fields.text: When preparing game you damage a work surface. You should:
fields.option_a: Repair it as soon as possible
fields.option_b: Wait until you get time before repairing the damage
fields.option_c: Wait until an EHO spots the damage
fields.option_d: Cover it up and hope that it won’t get spotted

fields.text: Which of the following do not meet the hygiene standards for game larders?
fields.option_b: A segregated hanging area, stainless steel fixtures and fittings
fields.option_c: Wooden handled knives and blocks
fields.option_d: A well ventilated brick building with good lighting

fields.text: How often should you check your larder temperature?
fields.option_c: Only during cold weather

fields.text: Why should larders have impervious ceilings, floors and walls?
fields.option_a: So you can write clearly on the wall
fields.option_c: To keep the larder cool
fields.option_d: So the larder can be easily cleaned and sanitized

fields.text: What material should be used for larder knife handles:
fields.option_c: Bone

fields.text: Which is the best method of cleaning a larder knife?
fields.option_b: Disinfect and dry with a towel
fields.option_c: Rinse and dry with a cloth
fields.option_d: Wash, sanitise, and leave to dry

fields.text: Larder tools and equipment are cleaned:
fields.option_a: After each use and before starting a new carcass
fields.option_c: At the beginning and end of the shooting season

fields.text: Cleaning equipment between uses:
fields.option_a: Is not necessary if tools have plastic handles
fields.option_b: Is not necessary if tools are made of stainless steel
fields.option_c: Reduces the risk of cross contamination
fields.option_d: Is not necessary if the larder is secure from pests and predators

fields.text: Whilst preparing a carcass in the larder, you drop your knife on the floor. You should:
fields.option_a: Pick it up and carry on regardless
fields.option_b: Wipe the blade on the carcass
fields.option_c: Wipe the blade clean on a disposable paper towel
fields.option_d: Wash and sanitise it before continuing, or replace it

fields.text: A game larder can be used to store:
fields.option_a: Carcasses and firearms and other shoot equipment
fields.option_c: Chemicals as long as game is not present
fields.option_d: Equipment and firearms but not controlled chemicals

fields.text: Smoking when working with carcasses:
fields.option_a: Good because it hides the smell
fields.option_b: Allowed, as long as the butts are disposed safely
fields.option_c: Not allowed as it creates a risk to food safety
fields.option_d: Good because it keeps the flies away from the carcasses

fields.text: You arrive at the larder with several carcasses. You should:
fields.option_a: Unload everything onto the ground outside the larder
fields.option_b: Unload everything onto the larder floor to sort it out
fields.option_c: Unload straight from the vehicle onto gambrels in the larder
fields.option_d: Put the game in the cold store in sacks to hang up later

fields.text: Which of the following statements is true?
fields.option_a: Fur and feathered game must be hung separately only if they were shot in different woods
fields.option_b: Fur and feathered game can be hung together in the larder
fields.option_c: You require two separate larders for fur and feather
fields.option_d: Fur and feathered game must be hung separately

fields.text: Which one of the following is a common food pest?
fields.option_c: Woodlice

fields.text: The most effective way for laying pest bait (poison) is:
fields.option_a: On saucers in the larder, so it is easy to see how much bait has been taken
fields.option_b: Outside the larder in approved bait boxes
fields.option_c: Inside the larder in a wire cage
fields.option_d: In plastic sachets inside the larder, so it is easy to see when the bags are missing

fields.text: Your larder door lets flies in through a gap by the hinges. You can best solve this problem by:
fields.option_a: Spraying the carcasses inside and out with fly spray
fields.option_b: Hanging up sticky fly papers near the carcasses
fields.option_c: Use a sealant in the gap to make the door fly-proof
fields.option_d: Giving a few squirts of fly spray into the air each time you close the door.

fields.text: Contamination caused by pests in a larder can be dealt with by:
fields.option_a: Leaving food outside the larder for the pests to eat
fields.option_b: Using cats to control the pests
fields.option_c: Good temperature control

fields.text: Indicate the main reason why pests are not allowed in a game larder:
fields.option_c: Pests can transfer food poisoning bacteria to food
fields.option_d: It is expensive to employ private contractors to get rid of pests

fields.text: When should the larder be cleaned?
fields.option_b: After each use and between batches of game
fields.option_c: At the beginning and end of the season

fields.text: Which of the following actions will help ensure that disinfectants are used effectively?
fields.option_b: Always store in a dark room
fields.option_c: Make them up as strong as possible
fields.option_d: Used in accordance with the label

fields.text: What should you use when cleaning the larder floor and walls?
fields.option_a: A strong-smelling disinfectant like Jeyes fluid
fields.option_b: A pine-scented toilet disinfectant like Harpic
fields.option_c: An approved food safe cleaner

fields.text: Disinfectants will do which of the following?
fields.option_c: Reduce the number of bacteria to a safe level

fields.text: If inappropriate cleaning agents are used in the larder, it may lead to:
fields.option_a: Game meat only being fit for your own consumption
fields.option_b: A public health risk if it enters the human food chain
fields.option_c: A lower financial return for the carcasses

fields.text: Your larder drain is smelly. You should:
fields.option_a: Flush it out well with clean water and an approved cleaning agent
fields.option_b: Pour a whole bottle of lavatory cleaner down it
fields.option_c: Use any brand of cleaner as long as it has a strong pine smell
fields.option_d: Use an air freshener until the weather gets colder

fields.text: Which one of the following is the correct method of storing cleaning materials?
fields.option_c: In a clearly marked cupboard
fields.option_d: On a window sill in the tool store

fields.text: How can you make sure that cleaning agents are used properly?
fields.option_c: Use double the concentration

fields.text: Which one of the following statements is true?
fields.option_c: Disinfectants cannot be used to sterilise a surface

fields.text: Cleaning chemicals which are not rinsed from food handling areas:
fields.option_a: Can contaminate the food and may be a risk to human health
fields.option_b: Will help to keep the area clean over a longer period of time
fields.option_c: Demonstrate you have cleaned the larder
fields.option_d: Will breakdown when in contact with meat

fields.text: Which one of the following statements best describes the action of a detergent?
fields.option_a: It kills all bacteria and cleans floors
fields.option_b: It removes grease and food debris but does not kill bacteria
fields.option_c: It sterilises worktops and leaves no smell
fields.option_d: It reduces the number of bacteria on worktops

fields.text: The main reason for the use of disinfectants in the game meat industry is to:
fields.option_a: Mask the smell of rotten game
fields.option_b: Kill all bacteria on work surfaces and equipment
fields.option_c: Reduce bacteria to a safe level on work surfaces and equipment
fields.option_d: Remove dirt, grease and food particles

fields.text: Disinfectants are used in food preparation areas to:
fields.option_b: Reduce bacteria to a safe level
fields.option_c: Reduce smells from drains

fields.text: After each use, larders should be cleaned to:
fields.option_a: Keep them clean in case the EHO visits
fields.option_b: To keep the game dealer happy
fields.option_c: To ensure they do not get rusty and last a lifetime
fields.option_d: To prevent bacteria being transferred to the next batch of game

fields.text: It is important to store larder waste in a sealed container, why?
fields.option_b: To reduce the risk of cross contamination
fields.option_c: To help keep overalls clean
fields.option_d: To keep the temperature of the waste down

fields.text: What is the minimum depth below ground that you should bury larder waste if permitted to do so on that site?
fields.option_c: 1m

fields.text: What should you do if you cut your finger whilst handling game?
fields.option_c: Stop work and go home
fields.option_d: Cover the cut with a waterproof blue coloured detectable dressing

fields.text: Why should you wash your hands frequently whilst handling game?
fields.option_c: To reduce the risk of cross contamination
fields.option_d: To prevent your hands getting sore

fields.text: Eating food of any type in the game larder is?
fields.option_a: Allowed if you wash your hands
fields.option_b: Poor hygiene practice and should not be allowed
fields.option_c: Allowed if you do not put food wrappers in the rubbish bin
fields.option_d: Allowed if you do not use the knives or other tools

fields.text: Your assistant has diarrhoea and you are short-handed. You should tell them to:
fields.option_a: Stay close to a toilet and do all the larder work
fields.option_b: Go out to the woods but take plenty of toilet paper with him
fields.option_c: Not to handle game or do any larder work
fields.option_d: Stop complaining, take a pill and wear plastic gloves when handling game

fields.text: You have a small open wound. You should:
fields.option_a: Wash the cut and apply antiseptic before dealing with game carcasses
fields.option_b: Put on a blue dressing and a plastic glove before handling any game
fields.option_c: Put on a bandage dressing to absorb the blood
fields.option_d: Ignore the wound, there is plenty of blood about anyway

fields.text: It is muddy and wet. When you get to the larder, what is the minimum precaution you should take?
fields.option_a: Get finished quickly even if your clothes and boots are very muddy
fields.option_b: Not touch any carcass until you have changed into white clothes and a hairnet
fields.option_c: Wash your boots outside and put on a clean apron
fields.option_d: Keep a set of old overalls in the larder to protect your clothes from blood

fields.text: When preparing carcasses in the larder, you should wash your hands and knife:
fields.option_a: On each occasion before you touch or cut exposed flesh
fields.option_b: Before you start and when you finish working on each carcass
fields.option_c: Only if they are very dirty
fields.option_d: Only if the deer had to be dragged during extraction and is muddy

fields.text: You are suffering from food poisoning on the day of a shoot. Which ONE of the following should you do?
fields.option_c: Take the day off

fields.text: If a gamekeeper has a boil on their arm what should they do?
fields.option_c: Work as normal
fields.option_d: Stay at work but wear a blue plaster over the boil and don’t handle the meat

fields.text: Why would you have to avoid handling game meat if a member of your household has food poisoning symptoms?
fields.option_a: Handling meat could trigger symptoms in you
fields.option_b: Bacteria from the meat could make the sick person’s symptoms worse
fields.option_c: You might need to leave work quickly
fields.option_d: You may be carrying food poisoning germs without showing symptoms

fields.text: Which one of the following is a common symptom of food poisoning?
fields.option_c: Sore throat

fields.text: Which one of the following is a common symptom of food poisoning?
fields.option_c: Tiredness

fields.text: Which one of the following statements about food law is correct?
fields.option_a: Only the owner of a food business can be prosecuted
fields.option_b: You do not have to tell your supervisor if you have diarrhoea
fields.option_c: You break the law if you do not maintain minimum standards of cleanliness at work
fields.option_d: You cannot break Food Law if you are self employed

fields.text: Which one of the following statements about food law is correct?
fields.option_a: Only needs to be registered as a food business if selling outside of the game season
fields.option_b: Any supply of processed game meat requires registration as a food business
fields.option_c: Only game dealers, shops and pubs selling food are considered food businesses
fields.option_d: You prepare game for your own consumption you must be registered as food business

fields.text: If you are delivering carcasses to an Approved Game Handling Establishment, what minimum principles should you meet for personal hygiene?
fields.option_a: You should observe the current regulations and codes of practice for personal hygiene while handling wild game
fields.option_b: You can only handle game you shot and transported
fields.option_c: Make sure you have wiped any blood off your hands before handling the carcass
fields.option_d: You do not need to abide by any regulations for export carcasses

fields.text: You have to change a wheel on your ATV before handling carcasses:
fields.option_a: There is no need to wash your hands
fields.option_b: Wash your hands in the nearest puddle
fields.option_c: There is no hygiene problem as long as the equipment used is clean
fields.option_d: You should wear clean plastic gloves whenever handling carcasses

fields.text: Protective clothing is worn in the larder to:
fields.option_a: Protect personal clothing from becoming spoiled with blood
fields.option_b: Make the larder workforce look professional
fields.option_c: Impress the Environmental Health Officer
fields.option_d: Prevent personal clothing being a source of contamination

fields.text: Hygiene regulations help ensure game larders maintain:
fields.option_c: The Working Time Directive

fields.text: The light in a game larder must be:
fields.option_b: Not less than 3 x 5 foot neon tubes
fields.option_c: Sufficient to inspect carcasses

fields.text: Which one of the following statements is correct?
fields.option_a: Food Laws protects the consumer from poor food hygiene practices
fields.option_b: Only large food companies have to have hazard analysis
fields.option_c: Food Laws do not apply to a stand selling hot dogs at a funfair
fields.option_d: Food laws only applies to medium-high risk food

fields.text: Which one of the following statements about bacteria is correct?
fields.option_b: All bacteria can be washed off food
fields.option_c: Bacteria grow best below 5ºC

fields.text: At which one of the following temperatures do food poisoning bacteria grow best?
fields.option_c: 37º C

fields.text: Which one of the following statements is true?
fields.option_a: All food businesses must be registered
fields.option_b: Lavatories must lead directly into food rooms
fields.option_c: Smoking is not allowed within 50 meters of a food production area
fields.option_d: All food handlers with less than 12 months experience need a medical before they start work

fields.text: Which one of the following is a legal requirement for a food business?
fields.option_a: They must only employ staff aged 18 or over
fields.option_b: Premises must be maintained in good condition and kept clean
fields.option_c: There must be a canteen serving hot drinks for breaks
fields.option_d: There must be a ‘NO SMOKING’ notice in the toilets

fields.text: A well designed game larder has:
fields.option_a: Sufficient room and light to handle/inspect game easily and store it
fields.option_b: Poor insulation which will not prevent game from heating up
fields.option_c: No door to allow easy access with trays and carcasses
fields.option_d: A wooden floor and walls so blood can drain away easily

fields.text: Hygiene regulations lay down measures to ensure that game transport:
fields.option_c: Has a limited environmental impact
fields.option_d: Stipulates minimum quantities of food transported

fields.text: When recovering game from the field to the larder, the best method of transport is:
fields.option_a: The quickest and cheapest to run
fields.option_b: The largest and best cross-country performance
fields.option_c: The one which gets the game home in best condition.
fields.option_d: The one which has the most traditional image

fields.text: It is a legal requirement for larder equipment & tools to be?
fields.option_c: Recorded

fields.text: Which of the following features of larder tools and equipment is controlled by food hygiene legislation?
fields.option_c: Appearance

fields.text: All work surfaces used in a game larder must be made of:
fields.option_a: Sanded and varnished planks of timber
fields.option_c: Solid beech blocks and sawn wood

fields.text: HACCP Plan (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point) should start :
fields.option_c: When the game arrives at the larder
fields.option_d: When game arrives at the game dealer

fields.text: Which one of the following statements about HACCP is true?
fields.option_a: It is primarily concerned with the management of food safety
fields.option_b: It is intended to reduce weekly working hours
fields.option_c: The purpose is to reduce accidents by game delivery vehicles
fields.option_d: It increases the value of wild game meat

fields.text: Which one of the following statements best describes ‘Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point system’ (HACCP)?
fields.option_a: HACCP seeks to identify food hygiene hazards and control them
fields.option_b: HACCP is mainly concerned with fire prevention and escape on the premises
fields.option_c: The entire HACCP system only needs to be reviewed once every 4 years
fields.option_d: HACCP is not suitable for use in game collecting centres

fields.text: Who must follow the HACCP principles?
fields.option_c: The local authority Environmental Health Officer
fields.option_d: Everyone involved in handling game carcasses

fields.text: Who must maintain records when placing a carcass into the human food chain?
fields.option_a: The Veterinary officer at an approved game dealer only
fields.option_c: Everyone who supplies game at each level
fields.option_d: The local authority Environmental Health Officer

fields.text: All game being supplied to an Approved Game Handling Establishment must first be inspected by:
fields.option_a: The land owner or representing agent
fields.option_c: A trained person
fields.option_d: The driver of the delivery vehicle

fields.text: Good record keeping is important for food safety because it enables:
fields.option_a: The accountants and tax man to detect fraud
fields.option_b: Customers to sue the suppliers of tough meat more easily
fields.option_c: The police to get written evidence to prosecute poachers
fields.option_d: Game to be traced both forward and back within the food chain

fields.text: Labelling of large game is required if it is being supplied to a game dealer - at what stage should this be undertaken?
fields.option_a: Immediately the deer is removed from where it was shot
fields.option_b: On arrival at the game larder
fields.option_c: When it is delivered to the game dealer’s door
fields.option_d: After inspection by a trained person

fields.text: If you sell a carcass to a game dealer with a declaration you have signed, but have not actually inspected the carcass or internal organs you have?
fields.option_a: Committed an offence under the Food Hygiene Regulations
fields.option_c: Sell carcass to be processed into burgers in the local hotel only
fields.option_d: To ensure you keep the best cuts of venison

fields.text: Traceability requires labelling of large game carcasses. At what stage should this be undertaken?
fields.option_a: As soon as the animal is found
fields.option_c: After inspection by a trained person
fields.option_d: On delivery to the game dealer

fields.text: All operators of food businesses must keep sufficiently detailed and accurate records to allow:
fields.option_a: Food to be traced from supplier to those supplied at each stage
fields.option_b: The weight loss in processing to be calculated
fields.option_c: County of origin to be put on the label

fields.text: The declaration completed by a trained person for a large game carcass after inspection and before sale must show:
fields.option_a: Name and address of trained person to whom payment should be made
fields.option_b: Weight, age and species of animal only
fields.option_c: Name of trained person, number, time, date, place of killing and declare it shows no abnormalities
fields.option_d: Name of trained person, temperature and weight before gralloching.

fields.text: As the only trained person on a shoot, you should:
fields.option_a: Ask the other hunters if the game was behaving normally before being shot and inspect all carcasses yourself
fields.option_b: Only inspect those carcasses where a problem is reported
fields.option_c: Require all game to be kept un-gralloched until the end of the day so you can do everything yourself at the same time
fields.option_d: Issue all hunters and assistants with your labels and tell them to inspect the carcasses and plucks themselves

fields.text: You are the only trained hunter on the estate but you have 2 assistants helping you with a deer cull. They may:
fields.option_a: Gralloch and inspect everything as long as they report to you any abnormalities found
fields.option_b: Gralloch the deer themselves but must retain the offal for your inspection
fields.option_c: Gralloch and dispose of the green offal if you have previously shown them how to inspect it
fields.option_d: Do nothing to the carcass without you being present

fields.text: A deer runs onto a frozen pond, falls through the ice and drowns. A trained hunter may:
fields.option_a: Retrieve it, inspect it and sell it to a dealer in the normal way after draining it of water
fields.option_b: Retrieve it, inspect it and give it away to the beaters but not sell it
fields.option_c: Retrieve it, inspect it, fire a shot into it and then sell to the dealer
fields.option_d: None of these – the carcass must not enter the food chain because it was not killed by a legal hunting method

fields.text: Your assistant is a good shot but not a trained hunter. You place him in a high seat and should tell him to:
fields.option_a: Note the animal's behaviour before shooting, gralloch but wait for you to inspect it.
fields.option_b: Gralloch the deer completely, leave the offal in the wood and drag out the carcass.
fields.option_c: Gralloch the green offal only and bury it
fields.option_d: Leave the un-gralloched carcass at the foot of the high seat and walk home.

fields.text: You find a game animal freshly dead by the side of a road. What can you do with the carcass?
fields.option_a: Inform the local authority so they can dispose of the carcass
fields.option_b: Take it home and eat it yourself
fields.option_c: Give it away only
fields.option_d: Sell it to a game dealer only

fields.text: You find a dead game animal with minimal damage lying by the roadside, what should you do?
fields.option_a: Sell it to a Food Business Operator
fields.option_b: Take it home to eat yourself
fields.option_c: Ensure that it cannot enter the food chain
fields.option_d: Take it home to feed your dog

fields.text: The correct disposal route for large amounts of game larder offal and waste products is to:
fields.option_a: Dispose of in a recognised legal manner
fields.option_b: Sell it as pig food to a farmer
fields.option_c: Put it in a dustbin for Council rubbish collection
fields.option_d: Take it back out to the woods for scavengers.

fields.text: For good food hygiene practice, when should waste be removed from the workplace?
fields.option_a: When the game has been collected by the dealer
fields.option_b: When the Environmental Health Officer is due to visit
fields.option_c: When it starts to get smelly
fields.option_d: When preparation of game for storage is complete

fields.text: If you suspect game has been exposed to veterinary medication what is your legal responsibility?
fields.option_b: Ensure the withdrawal period has been observed
fields.option_c: Bury in the woods and tell nobody
fields.option_d: You must inform all your friends that the meat may have side effects when eaten

fields.text: You manage a deer park and your best stag has to be darted with a no withdrawal period drug, under veterinary supervision. Two months (eight weeks) later he is shot. The carcass:
fields.option_a: Can go into the food chain immediately it has been inspected
fields.option_b: Can never go into the food chain
fields.option_c: Could have gone into the food chain 6 months (26 weeks) after treatment
fields.option_d: Could have gone into the food chain 1 month (4 weeks) after treatment

fields.text: If game has been recently treated with drugs, what is your legal responsibility?
fields.option_a: Ensure you observe any withdrawal period before it enters the food chain
fields.option_b: Shoot it and label it as dog food only
fields.option_c: Shoot it and bury in the woods and tell nobody
fields.option_d: Shoot it and attach a label stating that the meat may have side effects when eaten

fields.text: If game has been recently treated with drugs, where would you find relevant information?
fields.option_c: Social media sites

fields.text: If park deer have been recently treated with drugs prior to culling what is your legal responsibility as far a record keeping is concerned?
fields.option_a: Attach a label to the deer carcasses stating it has been recently treated with drugs and keep a copy of the label
fields.option_c: Maintain a record of the drugs administered and note the withdrawal period expiry date
fields.option_d: Tell the vet to record and report it

fields.text: Why is it important to keep records of any veterinary treatments you have administered?
fields.option_a: So that you can avoid treating more than you need to
fields.option_b: To be able to report the treatment to the FSA
fields.option_c: So you can let the Vet know how well the treatment worked
fields.option_d: So you can monitor withdrawal periods and ensure game meat is safe to eat

fields.text: If you suspect a notifiable disease you must?
fields.option_a: Label carcass as dog food only
fields.option_b: Report it to the Animal and Plant Health Agency at the earliest opportunity
fields.option_c: Bury carcass in the woods and tell nobody
fields.option_d: Warn all your friends the meat may have long term health effects if eaten

fields.text: If you have a carcass with a minor abnormality, what should you do?
fields.option_c: Make sausages out of the normal looking parts

fields.text: A carcass with a suspected notifiable disease must be:
fields.option_a: Hung up with others until disease is confirmed
fields.option_c: Left in the woods where it was culled

fields.text: The reason the law requires even suspicion of certain diseases to be reported to the Animal & Plant Health Agency is that:
fields.option_a: These diseases pose a risk to both human and animal health.
fields.option_b: Media action may be required to prevent a food scare
fields.option_c: The animal is suffering and it is cruel not to report it
fields.option_d: Researchers can only obtain their samples this way

fields.text: Who would you inform if you suspected a notifiable disease?
fields.option_c: Animal and Plant Health Agency

fields.text: If you report a suspicion of bovine TB to your local Animal and Plant Health Agency, the correct response from them is to:
fields.option_a: Tell you to preserve samples associated organs and hold the carcass for their inspection
fields.option_b: Order you to incinerate the carcass immediately
fields.option_c: Tell you to bury the carcass immediately
fields.option_d: Send you written permission to sell the carcass

fields.text: On inspection, you find symptoms which lead you to suspect TB. You should report the fact to Animal and Plant Health Agency and:
fields.option_a: Incinerate or bury the infected glands and pluck and then sell the carcass
fields.option_b: Keep the infected glands and associated organs in a plastic bag in a cool place and isolate the carcass for examination
fields.option_c: Sell the carcass and freeze the glands in a double plastic bag
fields.option_d: Incinerate or bury the whole carcass

fields.text: If you suspect a notifiable disease and inform the Animal and Plant Health Agency, what records must you keep?
fields.option_a: Weather, name of hunter and carcass sex and weight
fields.option_c: The game dealer keeps records, you need keep none
fields.option_d: A receipt from the game dealer

fields.text: If you suspect notifiable disease in game, you must?
fields.option_a: Collect and retain a receipt from the game dealer
fields.option_b: Record carcass details in your larder register
fields.option_c: Ask the game dealer to keep records, you need keep none
fields.option_d: Keep samples and carcass separate from other game and report to the Animal and Plant Health Agency

fields.text: Who has a responsibility to keep records in respect of carcasses supplied for human consumption?
fields.option_c: The Restaurant manager only
fields.option_d: Everyone who supplies game at whatever level

fields.text: The principle that all game must be traceable requires that large game should be individually labelled and that trays of small game should be labelled, when should this take place?
fields.option_a: When the carcasses are placed on the game-cart or transport after being shot.
fields.option_b: Immediately after being inspected by a trained person and before being placed in the larder.
fields.option_c: When being put on the transport for delivery to the game-dealer

fields.text: You have a large modern cold room/ chiller with separate hanging areas for large and small game so that cross contamination cannot take place. When you have both large and small game in the chiller, what is the maximum temperature at which it may be kept?
fields.option_c: 4C

fields.text: Carcasses of any game which have been handled in an unhygienic way must be;
fields.option_a: Carefully put with others to go to the dealer so that he does not notice the problem.
fields.option_b: Washed carefully to remove any contamination
fields.option_c: Always rejected so that they do not enter the human food chain.
